task_title: feat: add support for setting cursor result set position for multi-result queries
problem_statement: When multiple SQL statements are executed in a single query string, or when executemany() is called with returning=True, the psycopg cursor accumulates multiple result sets internally. Currently, the only navigation method available is nextset(), which advances exactly one result set at a time. There is no way to jump directly to an arbitrary result set by position. The cursor needs a set_result(index) method that accepts an integer index (supporting both positive and negative Python-list-style indexing), selects that result set as active, and raises IndexError when the index is out of range. The method should return self to allow call chaining.
problem_statement_variant: Users who run multi-statement queries or executemany() with returning=True cannot access a specific result set directly. They are forced to call nextset() repeatedly, which only moves forward one step at a time and provides no way to jump to a particular result set by index.
hints: The cursor already supports iterating over multiple result sets — see how nextset() works for the existing navigation pattern. A new set_result(index) method should allow jumping directly to any result set by index, supporting positive and negative indexing like Python lists, and raising IndexError when out of range. Both the sync Cursor and async AsyncCursor classes need this method.
